CLASSIFIED — FAMILY USE ONLY SUNDAY 19 APR 2026 / DEPART 10:30
Operation

Black River
Gorges

GRID: -20.3999 / 57.4667
ELEV GAIN: 693 M / 2,276 FT
DISTANCE: 12.4 KM LOOP
DURATION: 5 TO 5.5 HRS
CLASSIFICATION: HARD
PERSONNEL: 3
Mauritius's largest national park. Rainforest loop with waterfalls, river crossings, and gorge viewpoints. No signal on trail. All three of you come back.
Unit Member / Quiet Ops
Ryan
Quiet. Moves at his own pace. Notices things others miss. His silence is not a problem to solve.
SECRET WEAPON: AUTISM
Unit Member / Comms & Nav
Roy
Talks, questions, drives energy. Today that's an asset. His brain doesn't stop. Neither does the trail.
SECRET WEAPON: ADHD
Unit Member / Not In Command Today
Luna
High energy or shutdown, both are normal. Not the coordinator today. Just a hiker. The unit covers the rest.
SECRET WEAPON: AUDHD
SECTION 01 / Mission Timeline

Schedule of Operations

10:00
Kit Check / Everyone Packs Themselves
Each person checks the pack list below against their own bag. No one does this for anyone else. Car is at the door at 10:30.
10:30
Wheels Up / Tamarin to Petrin Information Centre
About 35 minutes. Route: B103 towards Plaine Champagne, then follow signs to Petrin. Google Maps and Waze both work. Search "Petrin Information Centre Black River Gorges." Free parking on site. Stop on the way if anyone needs breakfast.
ROY NAVIGATES
11:10
Advance to Trailhead
Park at Petrin Information Centre. Toilets on site, use them before you begin. Trail starts from the car park, signed. Check the offline map is working before you enter the forest.
11:30
Commence Loop / Macchabee Trail
This is a circular route, 12.4 km total. Tough climb at the start. Rainforest canopy almost the entire way, good shade. Muddy sections, exposed roots, some steep sections. Signal drops early, that is normal.
12:30
Machabee Viewpoint
First major landmark. Gorge views open up here. Eat something if you haven't. Water check. Rest as long as needed before continuing.
13:15
Plateau Remousse
Second viewpoint. Gorge panorama. The trail levels out more after this point. The hardest climbing is behind you.
14:00
Cascade Mare aux Joncs
Waterfall. River crossing shortly after. You can cool off in the water before the final stretch back. Watch footing on wet rocks.
15:00
Return to Trailhead / Petrin
Loop completes back at the car park. Drive home.
16:00
Mission Complete
Showers. Food. Done.
SECTION 02 / Terrain Intel

The Trail

HARD
Macchabee and Colophanne Trail Circular
12.4 kmLoop
5 to 5.5 hrsWalking time
693 mElevation gain
FreeEntry
Starts and ends at Petrin Information Centre. Circular route, no retracing steps. Tough climb at the start, then dense rainforest canopy almost all the way. Three key sights: Machabee Viewpoint (gorge panorama), Plateau Remousse (second viewpoint), and Cascade Mare aux Joncs waterfall with a river crossing near the end. Muddy in sections even in dry season. Marked trail, no guide needed. 71% of AllTrails reviewers rate this Hard.
If anyone needs to slow down or stop, say so. This is a long, hard loop. There is no shame in a slower pace. The unit moves at the pace of its slowest member, and that is the correct pace.
SECTION 03 / Navigation

Maps and Route

DRIVING: Google Maps and Waze both work. Search "Petrin Information Centre Black River Gorges" or "Petrin Heritage Trail Mauritius."
ON TRAIL: No signal. Navigate using the AllTrails offline map downloaded below. This is a loop, the route does not retrace. Follow the app.
Critical Pre-Mission Task / All Three of You
Download the Offline Map Before 10:30
There is no phone signal on this trail. The offline map is your only navigation once you are in the forest. This is not optional, and it is not one person's job. Each of you does this on your own phone. If one phone dies on trail, one of the others has the map. That is how a unit works.
  1. Download the AllTrails app (free) from your app store if you don't have it.
  2. Create a free account or log in.
  3. Open the link below and find "Macchabee and Colophanne Trail Circular."
  4. Tap Download on the trail page. The map saves to your phone.
  5. Confirm it works: go to airplane mode, open AllTrails, check the map loads. Then turn signal back on.
Open Trail on AllTrails
RYAN downloads own map
ROY downloads own map
LUNA downloads own map
SECTION 04 / Kit List

What to Bring

Each person packs their own bag / tap to check off

Trainers or hiking shoes
Closed toe. Not flip flops.
Water, full, whatever size you have
Ideal is 1.5L. No big bottle? Drink as much as you can before leaving, then bring whatever you have, full.
Mosquito repellent
Apply before entering the forest. Not optional.
Sunscreen
Viewpoints are exposed. Apply before you start.
Food for the trail
We buy it on the road. Small shops and bakeries en route to Petrin. Have some breakfast before or during the drive, even something small.
Phone, charged and offline map ready
AllTrails downloaded and confirmed in airplane mode before leaving.
Small backpack
To carry all of the above.
SECTION 05 / Rules of Engagement

How This Works

01
Roy navigates. Google Maps or Waze to the trailhead. AllTrails on the trail. Unsure of direction: check the app, check the markers.
02
Ryan sets the pace. No one rushes him. No one checks in. His silence is not an invitation to talk.
03
Luna is not the coordinator today. She hikes. If she goes quiet: leave her to it. The unit covers itself.
04
Stay within sight at all times. No need to walk side-by-side. But keep each other visible.
05
No signal is normal. Do not panic. You have the offline map. The trail is marked. Follow both.
06
Water check at the summit. Everyone drinks before starting the descent. Not negotiable.
SECTION 06 / Unit Protocols

A Strong Unit Covers Its Own

Why This Section Exists
A strong unit is not one where everyone operates the same way. It is one where each member knows what the others need and responds without being asked. Ryan, Roy, and Luna each have a different operating mode. None of those modes is a flaw. What follows is the field manual: what to look for, and exactly what to do. Read it once. Know it. Use it when it matters.
Luna
If Luna goes into shutdown
She will go quiet and slow down. She does not need fixing, entertaining, or checking on. Roy: say "take your time" and match her pace. That is it. Ryan: nothing required, keep moving at your own pace. Luna will come back online on her own. Do not make it a moment. The hike continues.
Roy
If Roy hits overload or starts to spiral
He will talk faster, jump between topics, or start catastrophising. Luna: give him one concrete fact. "We are on the trail. The app shows we are here. We keep going." One sentence. Do not match his energy, anchor it. Ryan: no action needed. Your calm already helps more than you know.
Ryan
If Ryan needs to stop or step back
He will slow or stop without explanation. That is the signal. Do not ask why. Do not offer solutions. Do not fill the silence. Find a flat spot nearby, stop, give him space. Wait. When he starts moving again, that is the all-clear. Luna and Roy: no words needed. Just wait.
OP ANAMO / BLACK RIVER GORGES NATIONAL PARK / MAURITIUS / 19.04.2026 / REDWOOD.LUNAMARGHERITA.COM/ANAMO
